授業目的:
The purpose of this
course is to explore Climate Justice – what it means, why climate change
is fundamentally a justice issue, and how its impacts and responsibilities are
distributed unevenly across societies and generations. Drawing on perspectives
and case studies from Canada, Japan, other global contexts, and the United
Nations, students will examine how climate change intersects with
inequality, policy, governance, and ethical responsibility.      Through interdisciplinary lectures, case studies, and
applied activities, students will engage with practical tools and frameworks
used in climate policy, sustainability governance, and ESG-related
decision-making. Students will be encouraged to reflect on climate justice in
their own social and national contexts, develop evidence-based arguments, and
propose informed and feasible pathways for climate justice action.
到達目標:
Students will be able to: 1. Understand and communicate key climate change and climate justice concepts; 2.Analyze global and local climate justice issues across different regions
and actors; 3. Evaluate
climate-related policies, initiatives, and organizational responses using justice-based frameworks and ethical reasoning; 4.Apply practical tools
for measurement, evaluation, and policy
analysis; and 5.Develop and present
reasoned proposals for climate justice action grounded in research, interdisciplinary perspectives, and
real-world constraints.
関連科目: Climate change science and policy; sustainability governance; social change; ecological economics; power and politics; human rights; migration and climate refugees; just transition; climate action
授業の概要・背景: Climate Justice requires us to examine the climate crisis through a human rights and equity lens. Climate Justice also challenges us to look for new and different ways to address existing inequities and to challenge the political and economic structures and power holders for changes to best facilitate a transition towards a just and sustainable society. Through interdisciplinary lectures, case studies, and applied activities, students will engage with practical tools and frameworks used in climate policy, sustainability governance, and ESG-related decision-making. Students will be encouraged to reflect on climate justice in their own social and national contexts, develop evidence-based arguments, and propose informed and feasible pathways for climate justice action.
授業方法: This course will be taught all in English (Advanced) with a mixture of lectures including in-person and on-line, and on-demand format with some group discussions. There will be practical tools introduced for use and analyses and sharing of researched ideas for action through presentations and/or paper readings. Class attendance, reading, writing, and participation in discussions is paramount as well as student participation in the peer evaluation process for the final presentations.

トピック.授業外学習2: Become more aware of your surroundings and the impact your decisions and actions may have on climate change. Access news and media reports for environmental-related topics and events for further study and class discussion.

学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student:
Peer reviews with opportunity for feedback and
discussions. The KGU Evaluation tool will also be used at the end of the term.

Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Global Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
Feedback will be givendirectly to students via grades and comments on assignments, writings/essays, and presentations.
